results.title: Hot wheels
results.creator_credit: Don McBrearty, Beryl Fox, McBrearty Film Productions and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A story where two girls turn down two boys for more prosperous dates. In anger, the teenage boys steal a car and after a drinking session accidentally meet up with the girls. The drivers of the two cars challenge each other to a race. A fictional film, dramatising a situation where pride, alcohol and wheels combine with disastrous results.

results.title: Hot stuff. The Ride. Dinosaur. Rip Van Winkle
results.creator_credit: Gerald Potterton, Will Vinton, Zlatko Grgic, Colin Low, Billy Budd Films, ONF | NFB and Will Vinton Productions
results.brief_description: A compilation of four excellent short films; two from The National Film Board of Canada and two from Will Vinton who pioneered clay animation. “Hot Stuff” (Canada, 1972, 10 min.): An award winning animated film about fire as the servant of man. “The Ride” (Canada, 1963, 7 min.): A slapstick comedy about a chauffeur for an eccentric tycoon chasing a runaway sled. “Dinosaur” (U.S., 1980, 14 min.): A clay animated interpretation of how earth existed before man. “Rip Van Winkle” (U.S., 1979, 29 min.) An adaptation of Washington Irving’s classic tale of a man who would rather tell stories than work in the fields.

results.title: Hot stuff
results.creator_credit: Zlatko Grgic, Robert Verrall, Wolf Koenig and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: This film traces man’s carelessness with fire, from its discovery to present day. Early man is warned that he must keep fire under control, but he gradually ignores this waring. Over-loaded sockets, smouldering cigarettes and other fire hazards result in the destruction of life and property. Story by Don Arioli. (Awards include: Melbourne, Toronto, New York, Atlanta, Zagreb, Chicago, Oberhausen.) Also available on DVD [319319].

results.title: Hot shots! (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Jim Abrahams, Bill Badalato, Peter Miller Investment Corp and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: A spoof of “Top Gun” in which Charlie Sheen stars as Topeer Harley, an ace fighter pilot who’s recruited to join a team of crack (or is it cracked?) jet jockeys for a top-secret mission Led by their incompetent commander (Lloyd Bridges). The squadron includes a half-blind pilot named ‘Wash-Out’ and a vain and smug golden boy. Together they train for operation ‘Sleepy Weasle’, a lightning strike against a desert kingdom. Meanwhile they spend their nights carousing in a nightclub where the beautiful airbase psychiatrist moonlights as a sexy chanteuse. To round out the insanity, a devious contractor is out to sabotage their mission.

results.title: Hot date
results.creator_credit: Kelly Wallwork, Dennis Tupicoff and Victorian College of the Arts
results.brief_description: Ingredients; one girl, one boy, and one Mexican cantina. The tale is of one woman’s experience of a date - the anxieties, trials and tribulations of trying to begin a relationship, which must inevitably begin with the first date. It doesn’t always go according to plan.

results.title: Hot curry cowboy
results.creator_credit: Justin Hutchison, Luigi Acquisto and Victorian College of the Arts
results.brief_description: A stranger from the West, Indian actor Billy “Butch” Bathshebas (Vejay Shanmugam), arrives in Australia fresh from the glitz of the Bombay studios. In correspondence to his father back home, Billy initially embellishes his flourishing cowboy life and acting career in Australia, that is until he lands a part in a chip commercial. On the set he meets a “real” cowboy, and the two are pitted in an ultimate showdown which has surprising results.

results.title: Hot air affair
results.creator_credit: Damon Smith, Greg Grainger and Grainger Maynard
results.brief_description: Follows the adventures of an all-female hot air ballooning team as they embark upon an international balloon race across Australia. The journey is not all smooth going, a few of the incidents include the balloon hitting a house and catching alight before finishing the spectaular, daring challenge.

results.title: Hot-dogger!
results.brief_description: A non-narrated look at the world of freestyle skiing. Shows serial flips, ballet and mogul skiing, and uses extreme slow motion to show skiers almost motionless inmidair as they fly thorugh single, double and Moebius flips. Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: Hostellers
results.creator_credit: Gloria Sachs, Edgar H. Anstey and British Transport Films
results.brief_description: The conversion of a canal barge into a youth hostel by voluntary working parties has created Britain’s first “hostel on water”. Two of the many youth hostellers involved give personal comments and observations on hostelling: how the hostels are run, the facilities offered, what hostelling means to them individually and how their lives are involved in it.

results.title: Hospital heroes
results.creator_credit: Ben Moller and Royal Children's Hospital and District Health Service Education Centre
results.brief_description: Going to hospital is often a frightening experience, and in the case of children - a traumatic one too. ‘Hospital heroes’ attempts to demystify this process as it follows three brave children who are going to hospital. Brooke is going into hospital to have a grommet put in her ear; Nicholas takes a trip in an ambulance after suffering an asthma attack; and Anthony has fallen off a bike and injured his head and leg and is carried away in a helicopter. The children are visited by “Agro” and a companion. A resource workbook (prepared by Christine Black) accompanies the video and provides guidance on curriculum ideas related to language, art, science, maths and social studies. Suitable for all primary levels as well as lower secondary level students in relation to mathematics.
